steps.title: Set up the tangent relationship from the right triangle
steps.content:
In a right triangle formed by the ramp, the angle $\theta$ with the ground satisfies:
$$\tan \theta = \frac{\text{height}}{\text{horizontal distance}} = \frac{29.1}{x}$$
The legal constraint requires $\tan \theta \lt \frac{1}{12}$, so:
$$\frac{29.1}{x} \lt \frac{1}{12}$$

steps.title: Solve for the minimum value of x at the boundary
steps.content:
The least possible value of $x$ occurs when the inequality becomes an equality:
$$\frac{29.1}{x} = \frac{1}{12}$$
Cross-multiply:
$$x = 29.1 \times 12 = 349.2$$
Any $x$ larger than 349.2 will make $\tan \theta$ smaller, satisfying the strict inequality. Therefore, the least possible value is 349.2 inches.
